Summary: Authentication Expansion
Description: Google-only auth limits reach. LinkedIn OAuth is a prerequisite for the direct integration epic. Magic link removes the need for a Google account entirely.

Summary: Add LinkedIn OAuth sign-in provider
Description: Add LinkedIn as an OAuth provider via next-auth. Prerequisite for the LinkedIn Direct Integration epic. Allows users to sign in with their LinkedIn account.

Summary: Add GitHub OAuth sign-in provider
Description: Add GitHub as an OAuth provider via next-auth. Broadens reach to technical users who prefer GitHub identity.

Summary: Add Magic Link / passwordless email sign-in
Description: Implement email-based magic link sign-in so users without Google or LinkedIn accounts can access the app. Use a secure token stored in the VerificationToken table (already exists in schema).

Summary: Remove email allowlist for open self-service sign-up
Description: Remove the ALLOWED_EMAILS env-var restriction to allow any user to sign up. Add optional domain-based restrictions as a replacement if needed. Currently blocks organic growth.

Summary: LinkedIn Direct Integration
Description: LinkedIn Optimizer currently outputs text the user must copy/paste manually. Direct publish via LinkedIn API removes that friction and is a meaningful differentiator no competing tool offers.

Summary: LinkedIn OAuth account connection (store access + refresh token)
Description: Allow users to connect their LinkedIn account via OAuth. Store access token and refresh token in a new LinkedInConnection model. Show connected/disconnected status on billing or profile page.

Summary: Import LinkedIn profile sections as a base resume
Description: After connecting LinkedIn OAuth, fetch profile data (summary, experience, education, skills) via LinkedIn API and convert it into a base resume in the app. Reduces onboarding friction dramatically.

Summary: Publish AI-generated headline directly to LinkedIn profile
Description: After generating a LinkedIn headline in the LinkedIn Optimizer tool, add a Publish button that writes the headline to the user's LinkedIn profile via the LinkedIn API using the stored access token.

Summary: Publish AI-generated About section directly to LinkedIn profile
Description: After generating a LinkedIn About section in the LinkedIn Optimizer tool, add a Publish button that writes the summary to the user's LinkedIn profile via the LinkedIn API.

Summary: Detect LinkedIn profile staleness vs last-optimized copy
Description: Track when a user last published to LinkedIn and when their base resume was last updated. Show a nudge on the LinkedIn Optimizer page if the published copy is older than the current resume.

Summary: Low Match Score Guidance
Description: Score <50% currently shows red and says try aggressive tailoring — not actionable. Users need to understand what is missing from their resume and why before they can close the gap.

Summary: Low match alert banner with targeted CTA
Description: When keyword match score is below 50%, show a prominent banner below the score bar with a clear call to action (e.g. See what's missing or Re-tailor with aggressive intensity). Visible to all plan tiers.

Summary: Gap Analysis card: AI identifies unaddressed JD requirements
Description: When score is below 50%, show a new Gap Analysis card below the keyword match card. Call Claude with the JD and tailored resume to identify which specific requirements are not addressed. Return a structured list of gaps with brief explanations. Gate to Starter+.

Summary: Actionable suggestions: specific bullets or sections to add
Description: Extend the Gap Analysis card with concrete suggestions: specific bullet points the user could write or sections they could add to close each identified gap. Gate to Starter+.

Summary: Skills to add list: JD requirements absent from resume
Description: Show a distinct list of skills or qualifications mentioned in the JD that are genuinely absent from the resume profile — not just missing from the tailored text. Help users identify real resume gaps vs tailoring gaps.

Summary: Pre-tailor role fit estimate before tailoring begins
Description: On the tailor form, after the JD is entered, show a rough fit estimate (e.g. ~40% match based on your resume) before the user submits. Helps set expectations and informs intensity selection. Use a fast Claude Haiku call on JD + resume summary.

Summary: Result Quality & Actionability
Description: Post-generation experience ends abruptly with no confirmation, no summary of what changed, and no clear next step. Small improvements here have high emotional impact.

Summary: Change summary: bullets rewritten and new keywords added
Description: After tailoring completes, compute and display a brief change summary: X bullets rewritten, Y new keywords added, Z sections reordered. Calculate by diffing base and tailored text server-side.

Summary: Success toast notification when generation completes
Description: Show a brief toast notification (e.g. Your tailored resume is ready!) when the tailoring API call resolves successfully. Use a lightweight in-app toast component.

Summary: User rating on tailored result (thumbs up/down + optional reason)
Description: Add a thumbs up / thumbs down rating widget at the bottom of the tailored resume result page. Store in a new TailoredFeedback table. Optionally collect a short reason (text select or free text). Feed signal into prompt iteration.

Summary: Apply to this job quick-link when JD URL was fetched
Description: When the user fetched the JD from a URL, show an Apply button on the result page that opens the original job posting in a new tab. Surfaces the most natural next action after viewing the tailored resume.

Summary: Email resume to yourself
Description: Add an Email to me button that opens a mailto link with the tailored resume text in the body and a pre-filled subject line. Useful for mobile users who want to copy/paste from their email client.

Summary: Cover Letter Enhancement
Description: Cover letter is generated once with no refinement options. Tone control and regeneration are table-stakes expectations in AI writing tools and increase perceived output quality.

Summary: Tone selector before cover letter generation
Description: Add a tone selector (Professional, Concise, Enthusiastic, Formal) before the user generates a cover letter. Pass the selected tone as an instruction to the cover letter prompt.

Summary: Regenerate cover letter with new tone or custom instructions
Description: Allow users to regenerate an existing cover letter with a new tone or additional instructions without navigating away. Add a Regenerate button alongside the existing cover letter output.

Summary: In-browser cover letter editing with auto-save
Description: Replace the read-only cover letter display with an editable contenteditable or textarea that auto-saves changes to the DB after a short debounce. Lets users refine the generated output without leaving the page.

Summary: Word count and estimated reading time indicator
Description: Show word count and estimated reading time (words / 200) below the cover letter output. Helps users keep it concise.

Summary: Job Application Tracker
Description: Users apply to multiple jobs simultaneously. Retold generates the resume but has no way to track what was sent where. A tracker extends the product session from generate to manage.

Summary: Application tracker table with linked tailored resumes
Description: Create a new /dashboard/applications page with a table of job applications: job title, company, status, date applied, and linked tailored resume. Add new JobApplication model to Prisma schema. Add Applications link to sidebar.

Summary: Application status workflow
Description: Add a status field to job applications with a dropdown: Applied, Phone Screen, Interview, Offer, Rejected. Allow users to update status inline in the tracker table.

Summary: Link tailored resume to application record
Description: When creating a new application entry, allow the user to link it to an existing tailored resume from a dropdown. Show the linked resume title in the tracker table with a link to the result page.

Summary: 7-day follow-up reminder banner
Description: 7 days after an application is marked Applied with no status update, show an in-app banner on the dashboard prompting the user to follow up or update the status.

Summary: Dashboard widget showing open applications in progress
Description: Add a stat card to the main dashboard showing the count of in-progress applications (Applied + Phone Screen + Interview). Links to /dashboard/applications.

Summary: Resume Intelligence
Description: The app analyzes the JD well but barely analyzes the resume itself. Quality signals at upload make the resume detail page more valuable and reduce poor-quality inputs to tailoring.

Summary: Action verb diversity score
Description: Analyze the verbs used in resume bullet points and display a diversity score. Flag overused verbs (e.g. managed used 5 times) and suggest alternatives. Show on the resume detail page.

Summary: Resume length feedback
Description: Detect approximate page count from the resume text and show a warning if it exceeds 2 pages. ATS systems and recruiters typically prefer 1-2 page resumes. Show on the resume detail page alongside ATS warnings.

Summary: Section completeness check
Description: Check that the resume includes expected sections (Experience, Education, Skills, Summary or Objective). Flag any missing sections as a soft warning on the resume detail page.

Summary: Skills gap trend: consistently missing skills across tailorings
Description: Aggregate missing skills from KeywordMatchLog for a user across all their tailored resumes. Surface a list of skills that are frequently missing — these are genuine resume gaps worth addressing.

Summary: Auto-suggest tailoring intensity based on pre-tailor match estimate
Description: When the pre-tailor role fit estimate is available (see Epic 3), pre-select the recommended intensity level on the tailor form. For example: estimate <30% -> recommend Aggressive, 30-60% -> Moderate, 60%+ -> Conservative.

Summary: Smart custom instruction suggestions from JD keywords
Description: When a JD is entered on the tailor form, suggest 2-3 pre-filled custom instruction snippets based on detected JD themes (e.g. leadership role detected: Emphasize your management experience). Users can click to insert.

Summary: Sharing & Export Expansion
Description: Users share resumes with mentors, career coaches, and recruiters. Current flow requires manual download and attachment. Frictionless sharing increases perceived value.

Summary: Shareable read-only link for tailored resume
Description: Generate a token-based shareable URL for a tailored resume that anyone can view without logging in. Token expires in 7 days by default. Add a Share button to the result page. New SharedResume model in Prisma.

Summary: Save to Google Drive
Description: Add a Save to Google Drive option in the download menu. Authenticate via Google OAuth (reuse existing Google provider) and upload the DOCX file directly to the user's Drive.

Summary: Save to Dropbox
Description: Add a Save to Dropbox option in the download menu. Use Dropbox OAuth and their file upload API to save the DOCX to the user's Dropbox.

Summary: Engagement & Retention
Description: Long-term retention via value reminders and social proof. Lower priority but meaningful lifetime value impact.

Summary: Monthly activity recap email
Description: Send a monthly email summary to active users: number of tailorings, average keyword match score, most-applied-to companies. Use a transactional email provider (e.g. Resend or SendGrid).

Summary: Re-engagement nudge after 7 days inactive
Description: If a user has not generated a tailored resume in 7 days, show an in-app banner on the dashboard (not email) prompting them to tailor a resume. Dismiss once clicked.

Summary: Achievement milestones
Description: Celebrate key moments: first tailored resume, 10th tailored resume, first 80%+ keyword match score. Show a one-time congratulations banner when a milestone is hit.

Summary: Referral program: refer a friend for free month credit
Description: Add a referral flow: users get a unique link, referred user signs up and upgrades, referrer gets one free month of their current plan. Use Stripe credit or manual override.

Summary: Agency & Team Workspaces
Description: Current Agency plan supports candidate labeling but not real team accounts. Agencies need shared workspaces with multiple seats and a client management view to scale their operations.

Summary: Team workspace with seat-based member access
Description: Introduce a Workspace model that multiple users can belong to. Agency plan owner can invite members. Members share access to resumes and tailored versions within the workspace.

Summary: Invite team members by email
Description: Workspace owner can invite colleagues by email. Invitees receive an email with an accept link. On accept they are added to the workspace with a member role.

Summary: Multi-seat agency billing with per-seat pricing
Description: Update Stripe billing for Agency plan to support per-seat pricing. Workspace owner pays for each active seat. Admin UI shows seat count and allows removing members.

Summary: Client and candidate management dashboard
Description: Add a Clients view for agency workspaces: group resumes and tailored versions by candidate. Show aggregate stats per candidate (resumes uploaded, tailorings done, avg match score).

Summary: Bulk tailoring: one resume against multiple JDs
Description: Allow users to submit a base resume against multiple job descriptions at once. Each JD generates a separate tailored resume. Results appear in a batch results view. Useful for agency recruiters applying to multiple openings.
